answer:
## Executive Summary
Lee Harvey Oswald had no direct connection to the CIA, as stated by the CIA itself in response to inquiries about his potential involvement with the agency [1]. The CIA had a file on Oswald, but it was primarily for informational purposes and did not indicate any operational relationship [2]. However, there are some inconsistencies and unanswered questions in the records regarding the CIA's interest in Oswald and his activities.

## Detailed Findings
### CIA's Statement on Oswald's Connection
The CIA explicitly stated that Lee Harvey Oswald was never associated or connected with the CIA in any way [1]. This statement is supported by the testimony of John A. McCone and Richard Helms before the Warren Commission [1]. The CIA also received copies of Oswald's manuscript from the FBI and the Warren Commission after the assassination [1].

### CIA's Interest in Oswald
Although the CIA did not have a direct connection with Oswald, the agency did have an interest in him due to his defection to the Soviet Union [2]. The CIA opened a 201 file on Oswald in December 1960, which served as a repository for records related to his activities and contacts [2]. The CIA also conducted investigations into Oswald's trip to Mexico City and his contacts with the Soviet and Cuban diplomatic missions [3].

### Investigations and Interviews
The House Select Committee on Assassinations conducted extensive interviews and reviews of CIA files related to Oswald [4]. The committee found that the CIA had conducted surveillance operations in Mexico City, which may have obtained photographs of Oswald, but the CIA denied the existence of such photographs [5]. The committee also interviewed Cuban citizens and Mexican citizens who may have had knowledge of Oswald's activities in Mexico City [6].

### Oswald's File and Records
The CIA had a single general file on Oswald, which was opened as a result of his defection to the Soviet Union [7]. The file grew to 58 volumes and contained all information in the agency's possession about Oswald and his family [7]. However, there is a mention of a sensitive HTLINGUAL document dated 8 July 1961, which was not included in the Oswald file due to its sensitivity [7].

### Polygraph Examination
The CIA conducted a polygraph examination of a Soviet defector, Nosenko, in October 1965, which included questions about Lee Harvey Oswald [8]. The results of the examination did not indicate any direct connection between Nosenko and Oswald [8].
